title: Club and Players Registries
text: ping package creates two main registries: Club registry: snapshot AFTT clubs metadata. Players registry (Messieurs): snapshot players points rankings registry can updated per month (one recording per month maximum otherwise overwritten) knowing players’ points updated monthly (first days month) AFTT. AFTT clubs likely updated yearly. registries latest snapshot, archive containing past snapshots stored.

title: Club and Players Registries
text: registries generated running two scripts build.club.registry.R build.players.registry.R (available within data-raw folder available github repository) build.club.registry.R creates new club registry snapshot AFTT annuaire https://data.aftt./annuaire/membres.php. process involves fetching club indices, names, provinces (one web page) scraping postal addresses team counts every club’s page. Merging consolidated club registry build.club.registry.R calls function: get.club.list.R build.players.registry.R retrieves Messieurs player list every club current club_registry. Iterate provinces clubs. Enriches data classement letters/numbers separation within-province ranking. build.players.registry.R calls functions: get.club.player.list.m

title: Club and Players Registries
text: Two datasets created: club_registry latest snapshot (data frame). club_registry_archive named list past snapshots. element named "YYYY_MM" contains data frame structure latest snapshot. See club_registry.R

title: Club and Players Registries
text: Similarly: players_m latest snapshot (data frame). players_m_archive named list past snapshots, also named "YYYY_MM".

what: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
title: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
text: Given two vectors representing old new classements, function: factorizes vectors using supplied classement order (already factors), computes contingency table transitions (transition_table), computes contingency table integer differences (diff_table).

title: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
code: classement.transitions(   old,   new,   ordre = c("NC", "E6", "E4", "E2", "E0", "D6", "D4", "D2", "D0", "C6", "C4", "C2", "C0",     "B6", "B4", "B2") )

title: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
text: old vector old/current classements (character factor). new vector new classements (character factor). ordre character vector giving classement order (default c("NC","E6","E4","E2","E0","D6","D4","D2","D0","C6","C4","C2","C0","B6","B4","B2")).

title: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
text: list three elements: transition_table Contingency table old vs new classement. diff_table Contingency table integer differences.

title: Build transition and difference tables for two classement vectors — classement.transitions
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   x<-c("D6","E6","E6","D6","E6","E6","D4","C2","B6","C0","D0")   y<-c("D6","E6","E6","D4","E6","E2","D0","C2","C0","C0","D0")   tables <- classement.transitions(old = x, new = y, ordre = ordre)   tables$transition_table   tables$diff_table } # }

text: data data frame containing player data. col_lettre Character. Name column containing classement letter (e.g. , B, C, D, E, NC). Default: "classement_lettre". col_chiffre Character. Name column containing numeric part classement. Default: "classement_chiffre". col_rk_inactive Character. Name column used identify inactive players. used actifs_only = TRUE. Default: "position_bis". actifs_only Logical. TRUE (default), filters rows col_rk_inactive == "Inactive" plotting. label_statut Character. Word displayed subtitle player count. Default: "Classement". y_label Character. Y-axis label. Default: "Pourcentage des actifs". ylim_max Numeric. Upper limit y-axis (proportion, percentage). Default: 0.4. fill_values Named character vector. Colours letter rank. Names must match levels present col_lettre. Default covers , B, C, D, E, NC. alpha_values Numeric vector. Alpha values mapped col_chiffre levels, controlling bar transparency. Default: c(1, 0.9, 0.8, 0.7, 1) (transparency NC's, B6/C6/D6/E6 transparent B0/C0/D0/E0)
